text.title: Go Standard Layout — Agentic Workflow and Prompts Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Go Standard Layout — Agentic Workflow and Prompts application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Pipeline MUST have four agents: layout / code-gen / wiring / review; never collapse to one prompt.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Internal agentic-workflow reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Every agent prompt MUST live in `.aidocs/prompts/` under version control.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: SDD methodology. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Layout agent MUST NOT emit business logic; only directories + placeholder files.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Internal reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Review agent MUST run `depguard` + `go vet` + tests before approving.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Internal QA gate. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Stack decisions (framework, ORM, error pkg) MUST live in `.aidocs/stack.md`; agents read instead of re-deciding.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: SDD methodology. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Wiring agent MUST emit dependency injection in `main.go`; never global singletons.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Hexagonal architecture. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Each scaffold step MUST be a separate git commit so failed stages can be reset cleanly.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Internal reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
